title: User, Agent, and Tool Authority, Least Agency, Dry Runs, and Revocation · Traceback Repair Bay
summary: A preserved agent trajectory contains one plausible model, schema, tool, authority, state, loop, memory, concurrency, injection, evaluation, telemetry, deployment, or recovery defect; locate the first failed invariant before editing. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in least-agency authorization decision engine and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evide…
objectives: Distinguish authenticated user, affected subject, agent, application, tool, service identity, resource owner, and approver.
Issue task-scoped, resource-scoped, time-bounded capabilities and expose only tools permitted for the current state.
Classify pure reads, sensitive reads, reversible writes, irreversible writes, external communication, financial, identity, and security effects.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-ai-agent-python cumulative safe evaluated AI agent portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python interaction, tool, policy, state, memory,
evaluation, telemetry, and recovery models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only.
Provider APIs, Gemini models, native google-genai, google-adk, Pydantic, HTTP, credentials,
host files, code execution, browsers, databases, telemetry backends, load, cost, privacy review,
and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class AgentPolicy:
    stakeholder_task_id: str
    allowed_tool_names: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_steps: int = 12
    maximum_tool_calls: int = 8
    approval_required_for_writes: bool = True


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class AgentEvidence:
    revision: str
    model_config_digest: str
    policy_digest: str
    evaluation_digest: str
    recovery_drill_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Safe evaluated Python AI agent portfolio ready"

steps.title: Safe AI agents and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Deny Escalate Revoke
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: agentpy-deny-escalate-revoke-i0mwxp" that uses case 9476 to separate user, agent, and tool authority with least privilege, effect classes, dry runs, denials, approvals, and revocation. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not contact provider APIs, invoke Gemini models, import or claim native google-genai, google-adk, or Pydantic behavior, open sockets, read or write host files, use credentials, execute generated code, launch browsers, reach databases or telemetry backends, or claim live privacy, load, safety, cost, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, google-genai 2.11.0, google-adk 2.4.0, Pydantic 2.13.4, fixed provider fixtures, fake transports, controlled local services, disposable cloud projects, representative eval sets, accessibility, privacy and security review, load, canary, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Safe AI agents and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Deny Escalate Revoke.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Deny Escalate Revoke into observable performance evidence. For Safe AI agents and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Deny Escalate Revoke, keep Deny Escalate Revoke connected to executable Python model, interaction, tool, policy, state, approval, evaluation, security, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: agentpy-deny-escalate-revoke-i0mwxp
# Competency: agentpy-deny-escalate-revoke.
# Case 9476: a keyboard and screen-reader interaction review.
# Operating constraint: every external effect requires explicit least-authority policy.
# Changed case: replace trusted context with untrusted indirect instructions and preserve data-versus-instruction boundaries.
def evidence_agentpy_deny_escalate_revoke_i0mwxp(requested_actions: int = 24, authorized_actions: int = 10, denied_actions: int = 8, approval_actions: int = 6, privilege_leaks: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_i0mwxp = "i0mwxp-pxwm0i-9476"
    assert evidence_variant_i0mwxp
    evidence_axes_i0mwxp = (
        "authorityi0mwxp",
        "interaction9476",
        "toolpxwm0i",
        "statei0mwxp9476",
        "evaluation9476i0mwxp",
        "recoverypxwm0i9476",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_i0mwxp)) == 6
    if requested_actions < 1 or authorized_actions < 0 or denied_actions < 0:
        return False, requested_actions, "authorization-count"
    if authorized_actions + denied_actions + approval_actions != requested_actions:
        return False, authorized_actions, "authorization-reconciliation"
    if approval_actions < 1:
        return False, approval_actions, "approval-boundary"
    if privilege_leaks > 0:
        return False, privilege_leaks, "privilege-leak"
    assert authorized_actions + denied_actions + approval_actions == requested_actions and privilege_leaks == 0
    return True, authorized_actions, "least-agency-policy"
